title: Promanage
tagline: Full-stack project management platform on the MERN stack
description: A project management system for tracking tasks, user roles, and workflows — built with the MERN stack and TypeScript, Redux Toolkit for state, and Tailwind for the UI.
problem: Small teams need a lightweight, opinionated way to plan work, assign roles, and track workflow without paying for enterprise tooling.
architecture: A typed REST API in Node.js + Express talking to MongoDB via Mongoose, with a React + TypeScript frontend. Redux Toolkit owns server-cache state, with a Tailwind-driven UI and role-based access enforced both client- and server-side.
features: Task management with assignees, due dates, and status workflows
User roles and permission-aware UI
RESTful API for tasks, users, and workflow tracking
Scalable MongoDB schemas tuned for the dashboard query shape
Responsive Tailwind UI
challenges: Keeping the API surface coherent as features grew
Modeling workflow transitions without leaking enforcement to the client
solutions: Defined typed contracts shared by client and server to keep the API consistent
Centralized workflow rules in a single service layer, exposed via narrow endpoints
optimizations: Composite indexes on the dashboard query shape
Selective Redux subscription to minimize re-renders

title: Woco HRMS Platform
tagline: Multi-module HRMS on the MERN stack
description: A production HRMS at Woco — Document Directory, Offboarding, and Expense Reports modules — designed for the full employee lifecycle with role-based access and audited workflows.
problem: Internal HR workflows were fragmented across tools — document handling, offboarding, and expense reporting needed a single, role-aware platform.
architecture: Modular MERN architecture with a shared auth/RBAC layer. Express services expose REST APIs per module, MongoDB stores documents with version metadata, and the React frontend renders role-scoped views from a unified design system.
features: Document Directory with structured metadata and access control
Offboarding workflow with task checklists and approvals
Expense Reports with attachment handling and review flows
Role-based access enforced server-side, mirrored in the UI
Audit-friendly event logging
challenges: Modeling distinct workflows without forking the auth and audit stack
Designing schemas flexible enough for future modules
solutions: Extracted a shared workflow engine and reused it across modules
Introduced a metadata-first document model that downstream modules can extend
optimizations: Indexed compound fields used in scope-checks to keep RBAC fast
Lazy module routes to keep initial JS small

title: Performance Management System (PMS)
tagline: Structured employee evaluation workflows on MERN + TypeScript
description: A PMS module at Woco enabling configurable review cycles, goal tracking, and evaluation workflows — built end-to-end with strongly-typed APIs and a schema designed for long-term reporting.
problem: Performance reviews ran in spreadsheets and email — slow, inconsistent, and impossible to report on at scale.
architecture: REST APIs in Node.js/Express expose configuration, goal tracking, and review endpoints; MongoDB schemas are designed for both the operational flow and downstream reporting. A typed shared contract drives the React client.
features: Configurable review cycles and rubrics
Goal tracking with progress checkpoints
Manager / employee / HR review flows
Typed API contracts shared with the frontend
challenges: Modeling reviews so historical cycles remain queryable as the rubric evolves
Coordinating multi-party review state without race conditions
solutions: Versioned rubric snapshots stored alongside each cycle
State machine governing transitions, with idempotent endpoints for each step
optimizations: Aggregation pipelines for the reporting dashboard
Indexes tuned for cycle + employee lookups

title: Shift Week Roster
tagline: Smarter shift scheduling — 40% less manual planning
description: A scheduling module that replaced a manual spreadsheet workflow with a structured weekly roster — cutting planning time by 40% and reducing scheduling conflicts.
problem: Weekly shift planning was a spreadsheet-driven manual process — slow, error-prone, and hard to audit when conflicts arose.
architecture: A dedicated scheduling service in Node.js handles roster generation and validation; constraint checks run server-side, and a React grid renders the editable weekly view. State is normalized in MongoDB with indexes optimized for week- and employee-level reads.
features: Drag-friendly weekly grid with employee + shift dimensions
Real-time conflict and overlap detection
Bulk operations across weeks and employees
Audit trail of changes
challenges: Detecting overlaps and rule violations without bottlenecking edits
solutions: Validation moved to a per-cell server check with debounced batching
optimizations: Indexed compound keys on (week, employee) for fast roster reads

title: Jira ↔ Woco Support Integration
tagline: API-based sync between Jira and an internal support system
description: A bi-directional sync between Jira and Woco's internal support system — keeping tickets, status, and comments coherent across both tools and improving issue visibility for the team.
problem: Issues were tracked in two places — Jira and Woco's support system — leading to duplicated work and inconsistent status across the team.
architecture: An integration service consumes Jira webhooks and Woco events, normalizing them through a sync engine. Idempotency keys and version vectors prevent loops and conflicting updates.
features: Two-way sync of tickets, status, and comments
Idempotent event handlers with retry and dead-letter queue
Configurable field mapping per project
challenges: Preventing echo loops when both systems emit change events
solutions: Per-record version vectors plus source-tagging to short-circuit re-emits
optimizations: Coalesced bursty webhook traffic into batched writes

title: Somersault & Aquatan — Shopify Storefronts
tagline: Shopify development for sister DTC brands
description: Led Shopify development for Woco's sister brands Somersault and Aquatan — building dynamic sections, shipping new features, and fixing critical bugs to lift performance and storefront UX.
problem: Two DTC storefronts shared similar mechanics but each needed dedicated dynamic sections and feature work — without slowing pages down.
architecture: Modular Liquid sections backed by metafield-driven content, paired with a lean JS layer for interactivity. Reusable schema blocks make merchandising changes safe in the theme editor.
features: Custom dynamic sections (heroes, story blocks, product cards) editable from the theme editor
Metafield-driven content for richer PDPs
Performance and bug fixes across both storefronts
challenges: Sharing logic across two themes without coupling them
solutions: Extracted shared snippets behind small adapters per brand to keep theming independent
optimizations: Deferred non-critical JS, image preloading on PDP, and section-level lazy rendering
